comment.body: my hedge fund buddies always say, bull markets are the hardest for them bc making money is not enough. investors want out performance. but in a bear market, redemptions dont hit as hard if you lose more than others (unless its a ridiculous amount) as long as you performed well in the bull. people are OK to lose when everyone else is losing. not OK to underperform when everyone is winning

comment.body:
nobody missed anything. 
there will always be waves that you will miss, and then what?
never let it stick into your mind. move on, and check whats ahead of you.
the biggest blunder is to trade without concenption.
if you did a rational decision and was wrong, you did better mentally, than chasing and accidentaly win.

in the first case you will win on the long run, on the second case you will lose everything.
marathon. no sprinting

comment.body:
This in combination with options flow, over sold conditions, and very lucrative PE ratios should’ve given enough context for a swing higher, especially if there was an incentive to prop up the market heading into ME meetings.

Of course, so much emphasis was put on Russia/Ukraine, that has now seemingly disappeared. To put the entire stake of the tariffs on the Ukraine/Russia conflict seems to be very misguided. And lead to being too conservative when we were trading 1,000 points lower.

Getting caught into these subjective narrative driving feedback loops and taking your eyes of the hard data hasn’t done this Substack any justice.
